transcript:
HCP: hi there, whats going on
Patient: hello doctor, i have this nasty cough that wont go away
HCP: how long has it been going on
Patient: about a week now, started after i caught a cold
HCP: any wheezing or chest tightness
Patient: yeah, feels like i cant breathe properly when i exercise
HCP: ok, let me listen to your lungs
Patient: okay
HCP: breathing sounds clear but there is some wheeze on the left
HCP: we should check your peak flow and give you some ventolin
Patient: thanks doctor
HCP: also, your blood pressure was 140 over 90 yesterday, need to keep an eye on that
Patient: i try to take my meds but sometimes forget
HCP: i will write down a new schedule for you
Patient: thank you

hpi.text: cough for a week, started after cold, wheezing and chest tightness when exercising
hpi.evidence_span: about a week now, started after i caught a cold

exam.evidence_span: there is some wheeze on the left

items.evidence_span: check your peak flow and give you some ventolin
i will write down a new schedule for you

transcript:
HCP: hi there, whats the matter?
Patient: so i woke up with a terrible headache today
HCP: how long has this been going on?
Patient: just this morning, really sharp
HCP: any history of migraines or high blood pressure?
Patient: no history of migraines but my BP was a bit high last time i checked
HCP: ok, lets check your BP, looks like 145 over 90 today
Patient: yep that feels about right
HCP: sounds like tension headache maybe, try some panadol and rest
Patient: thanks doc

hpi.text: headache started this morning, sharp pain
hpi.evidence_span: woke up with a terrible headache today

transcript:
HCP: hi, whats bringing you in today?
Patient: yeah, so my knee been hurting pretty bad for a while now
HCP: how long have you had it?
Patient: about 2 weeks i suppose, it starts when i walk up stairs
HCP: any swelling or redness?
Patient: a bit of swelling yeah
HCP: any history of arthritis?
Patient: not really, just some old sports injuries
HCP: right, thanks for telling me
HCP: lets have a look, knee looks slightly swollen but no redness
Patient: feels stiff in the morning
HCP: ok, sounds like a bit of osteoarthritis flare, try some ibuprofen if you can take it, and ice it
Patient: thanks doc

hpi.text: knee hurting for 2 weeks, worse with stairs, slight swelling, stiff in morning
hpi.evidence_span: my knee been hurting pretty bad for a while now

exam.evidence_span: knee looks slightly swollen but no redness

transcript:
HCP: hi there, how are you feeling today
Patient: um hi doc, i'm feeling a bit rough actually
HCP: what seems to be the trouble
Patient: well i've got this really bad knee pain on the right side and it's been throbbing for about a week now
HCP: right, where is the pain exactly
Patient: right below my kneecap, right there
HCP: any swelling or redness you've noticed
Patient: yeah there's a bit of swelling and it feels warm to the touch
HCP: okay, have you done any sports recently
Patient: not really, just some light gardening but it started after i bent down to pick up a tool
HCP: did you have any falls or injuries
Patient: no nothing like that, just the bending i suppose
HCP: any history of arthritis in your joints
Patient: no never had that before, just this one issue
HCP: right let me have a look, can you lift your skirt and bare your knee
Patient: sure no problem
HCP: okay, looks like there's some effusion and the patella is quite tender to palpation
HCP: i'm going to prescribe you some ibuprofen and maybe a corticosteroid injection to settle this down
HCP: also i'll give you a prescription for physiotherapy to help strengthen the area
HCP: any other medications you're currently taking
Patient: uh just some panadol for headaches sometimes
HCP: allergies
Patient: uh i think maybe penicillin but not sure, i've had rashes before with antibiotics
HCP: ok let's list penicillin as an allergy just to be safe
HCP: anything in your past medical history i should know about
Patient: no just high blood pressure which i manage with meds
HCP: which medication is that for your blood pressure
Patient: lisinopril
HCP: right, take that with the ibuprofen and rest the knee for a few days
HCP: come back next week if the swelling hasn't gone down or if you're still in pain
Patient: thanks doc, much appreciated
HCP: no worries, feel better soon

chief_complaint.evidence_span: bad knee pain on the right side

hpi.text: throbbing pain for a week, swelling and warmth, started after bending down, no falls or injuries
hpi.evidence_span: it's been throbbing for about a week now

meds.evidence_span: just some panadol for headaches sometimes

exam.text: effusion and patella tender to palpation
exam.evidence_span: looks like there's some effusion and the patella is quite tender to palpation

transcript:
HCP: hi there, what seems to be the trouble today?
Patient: hello doc, i've been feeling really tired for about a week now, just generally knackered
HCP: any specific symptoms?
Patient: yeah, my knee's been hurting pretty bad too, can't really bend it without a twinge
HCP: ok, any history of arthritis or joint replacements?
Patient: no, never had any issues like that before
HCP: got it down then, any current meds?
Patient: just taking some ibuprofen for the pain
HCP: allergies?
Patient: uh, latex
HCP: right, let me have a look, knee looks swollen and tender to touch
HCP: sounds like a minor sprain combined with fatigue, maybe catching a bug, ibuprofen for the pain, ice packs, physio if it doesn't settle in a few days

chief_complaint.evidence_span: feeling really tired for about a week now, just generally knackered

hpi.text: tired for about a week, knee hurting, can't bend without twinge
hpi.evidence_span: feeling really tired for about a week now, just generally knackered

meds.evidence_span: just taking some ibuprofen for the pain

exam.text: knee swollen and tender to touch
exam.evidence_span: knee looks swollen and tender to touch

problems.name: fatigue
problems.evidence_span: sounds like a minor sprain combined with fatigue

items.evidence_span: physio if it doesn't settle in a few days

transcript:
HCP: hi there, whats bothering you today?
Patient: uh so i've got this really bad knee pain, like i can't walk properly
HCP: where exactly?
Patient: right knee, feels like its tearing up every step i take
HCP: how long has this been going on?
Patient: since last monday, been getting worse all week
HCP: any swelling or redness?
Patient: yeah, there's some swelling near the joint and it feels hot to the touch
HCP: any history of knee injuries?
Patient: i fell off my bike about six months ago, twisted it pretty bad
HCP: any other conditions?
Patient: i have high blood pressure, taking amlodipine
HCP: any allergies?
Patient: allergic to penicillin
HCP: ok let me have a look, knee looks swollen and warm
HCP: range of motion?
Patient: can only bend it like halfway before it hurts
HCP: sounds like a meniscus tear or inflammation, i'll order an xray
HCP: keep taking your bp meds, ice the knee, no running for now
HCP: return if fever develops
Patient: thanks doc

chief_complaint.evidence_span: right knee, feels like its tearing up

hpi.text: pain since last monday, worsening, swelling and heat, limited flexion
hpi.evidence_span: since last monday, been getting worse all week

exam.text: knee swollen and warm, limited flexion

problems.name: meniscus tear or inflammation
problems.evidence_span: sounds like a meniscus tear or inflammation

transcript:
HCP: hello, what seems to be the problem?

HCP: 140 over 90

HCP: ok, knee sounds like a soft tissue sprain, cough is viral

transcript:
HCP: hi, good morning
Patient: yeah, hi doctor
HCP: what seems to be the problem today
Patient: well, my knee's been giving me a bit of a hard time lately
HCP: where exactly is the pain
Patient: right knee, mostly the front bit
HCP: how long has this been going on
Patient: probably about two weeks now, started after I went for a jog
HCP: any swelling or warmth
Patient: a little bit of swelling, not too bad though
HCP: let me have a look, okay, mild effusion, tenderness to palpation
HCP: any history of knee injuries
Patient: not really, maybe a twist a few years back but nothing major
HCP: any other medical conditions or medications
Patient: just high blood pressure, on amlodipine
HCP: any allergies
Patient: no, none that I know of
HCP: okay, sounds like a mild meniscal irritation or early arthritis flare
HCP: try ice packs twice a day and rest the leg
HCP: ibuprofen if it's bothering you, unless you've got stomach issues
HCP: physio might help if it doesn't settle in a week
HCP: come back if it gets worse or you can't walk

chief_complaint.evidence_span: my knee's been giving me a bit of a hard time lately

hpi.text: pain for two weeks after jogging, slight swelling
hpi.evidence_span: probably about two weeks now, started after I went for a jog

exam.evidence_span: okay, mild effusion, tenderness to palpation

problems.name: meniscal irritation or early arthritis flare
problems.evidence_span: sounds like a mild meniscal irritation or early arthritis flare

items.evidence_span: try ice packs twice a day
come back if it gets worse

transcript:
HCP: good morning, whats the issue?
Patient: hi doc, my ankle is really swollen and painful
HCP: where exactly?
Patient: left ankle, been hurting since the weekend
HCP: any injury?
Patient: twisted it playing soccer
HCP: ok, lets look, ankle is swollen and warm
HCP: x-ray shows no fracture, looks like sprain
HCP: ice it, rest the leg, pain relief if needed
HCP: follow up in a week if not better

hpi.text: twisted ankle playing soccer since weekend
